rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and component features in AGREE expressions; avoid referencing AADL properties as variables.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Place implementation-level assignments in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections are defined.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;`, `guarantee "label": expression;`, `eq name : type = expression;`, and `const name : type = value;`.
Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees for ports; implementation contracts handle bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assign clauses in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL timing properties like Deadline or Period directly in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scope in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
